title: Digestion BclI
steps: Add: - nuclease-free water qsp50 µL > 43µL - 10X Buffer G 5 µL - 1µg DNA (1µg/µL) - 1 µL BclI 10U/µL > 10U/1µg DNA Mix gently and spin down for a few seconds. **Star Activity : An excess of BclI (20 U/µg DNA x 1 hour) may result in star activity
Incubate at 55°C for 1-16 hours**. It's possible to use extended digestion for 16 hours by adding 0.5U for 1 µg of DNA in 50 µL 01:30:00
- Inactivated at 80°C in 20 min. > For only 10 U of enzyme or - adding 0.5 M EDTA, pH 8.0 (#R1021), to achieve a 20 mM final concentration

1. Measure the volume of the DNA sample. > 50 µL 2. Add 1/10 volume of sodium acetate, pH 5.2, (final concentration of 0.3 M) - These amounts assume that the DNA is in TE only; if DNA is in a solution containing salt, adjust salt accordingly to achieve the correct final concentration.> 5 µL 3. Mix well. 4. Add 2 to 2.5 volumes of cold 100% ethanol (calculated after salt addition).> 110 (2V) 5. Mix well. 6. Place on ice or at -20 degrees C for >20 minutes. 7. Spin a maximum speed in a microfuge 10-15 min. 8. Carefully decant supernatant. 9. Add 1 ml 70% ethanol. Mix. Spin briefly. Carefully decant supernatant. 10. Air dry or briefly vacuum dry pellet. 11. Resuspend pellet in the appropriate volume of TE or water.
creator: Antoine Ziller
